payload.text:
The user wants me to record something in the memory system following the AGENTS.md memory protocol. They want:
1. A memory with type=lesson, name=writer1-chat-proof
2. Body explaining this is the first chat-driven memory written by an LLM session following the prose contract in AGENTS.md
3. Drop a single sticky note JSON in .kody/memory/inbox/
4. Commit it

Let me first check the AGENTS.md file to understand the memory protocol, and also look at the existing memory system structure.

payload.content:
1 # Payload CMS Development Rules 2 3 You are an expert Payload CMS developer. When working with Payload projects, follow these rules: 4 5 ## Core Principles 6 7 1. **TypeScript-First**: Always use TypeScript with proper types from Payload 8 2. **Security-Critical**: Follow all security patterns, especially access control 9 3. **Type Generation**: Run `generate:types` script after schema changes 10 4. **Transaction Safety**: Always pass `req` to nested operations in hooks 11 5. **Access Control**: Understand Local API bypasses access control by default 12 6. **Access Control**: Ensure roles exist when modifying collection or globals with access controls 13 7. **Payload-First**: Always use Payload's built-in URL utilities and API endpoints before creating custom implementations 14 15 ### Code Validation 16 17 - To validate typescript correctness after modifying code run `tsc --noEmit` 18 - Generate import maps after creating or modifying components. 19 20 ## Project Structure 21 22 ``` 23 src/ 24 ├── app/ # Next.js App Router 25 │ ├── (frontend)/ # Frontend routes 26 │ ├── (payload)/ # Payload admin routes 27 │ └── api/ # API routes 28 ├── client/ # Client-side hooks, state, utils 29 ├── infra/ # Infrastructure (analytics, auth, blob, LLM, config) 30 ├── server/ # Server-side code 31 │ ├── payload/ 32 │ │ ├── collections/ # Collection configs 33 │ │ ├── globals/ # Global configs 34 │ │ ├── hooks/ # Hook functions 35 │ │ ├── access/ # Access control functions 36 │ │ ├── endpoints/ # Custom endpoints 37 │ │ └── jobs/ # Background jobs 38 │ └── services/ # Business logic services 39 ├── ui/ # React components 40 │ ├── admin/ # Payload admin UI components 41 │ ├── cody/ # Cody pipeline components 42 │ └── web/ # Frontend/consumer UI components 43 ├── i18n/ # Internationalization 44 ├── types/ # Type declarations 45 └── payload.config.ts # Main config 46 ``` 47 48 > **Note**: Do NOT create a `lib/` folder under `src/`. Place shared utilities in appropriate domain-specific directories (e.g., `src/ui/cody/`, `src/server/services/`, `src/infra/`). 49 50 ## Configuration 51 52 ### Minimal Config Pattern 53 54 ```typescript 55 import { buildConfig } from 'payload' 56 import { mongooseAdapter } from '@payloadcms/db-mongodb' 57 import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical' 58 import path from 'path' 59 import { fileURLToPath } from 'url' 60 61 const filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) 62 const dirname = path.dirname(filename) 63 64 export default buildConfig({ 65 admin: { 66 user: 'users', 67 importMap: { 68 baseDir: path.resolve(dirname), 69 }, 70 }, 71 collections: [Users, Media], 72 editor: lexicalEditor(), 73 secret: process.env.PAYLOAD_SECRET, 74 typescript: { 75 outputFile: path.resolve(dirname, 'payload-types.ts'), 76 }, 77 db: mongooseAdapter({ 78 url: process.env.DATABASE_URL, 79 }), 80 }) 81 ``` 82 83 ## Collections 84 85 ### Basic Collection 86 87 ```typescript 88 import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload' 89 90 export const Posts: CollectionConfig = { 91 slug: 'posts', 92 admin: { 93 useAsTitle: 'title', 94 defaultColumns: ['title', 'author', 'status', 'createdAt'], 95 }, 96 fields: [ 97 { name: 'title', type: 'text', required: true }, 98 { name: 'slug', type: 'text', unique: true, index: true }, 99 { name: 'content', type: 'richText' }, 100 { name: 'author', type: 'relationship', relationTo: 'users' }, 101 ], 102 timestamps: true, 103 } 104 ``` 105 106 ### Auth Collection with RBAC 107 108 ```typescript 109 export const Users: CollectionConfig = { 110 slug: 'users', 111 auth: true, 112 fields: [ 113 { 114 name: 'roles', 115 type: 'select', 116 hasMany: true, 117 options: ['admin', 'editor', 'user'], 118 defaultValue: ['user'], 119 required: true, 120 saveToJWT: true, // Include in JWT for fast access checks 121 access: { 122 update: ({ req: { user } }) => user?.roles?.includes('admin'), 123 }, 124 }, 125 ], 126 } 127 ``` 128 129 ## Fields 130 131 ### Common Patterns 132 133 ```typescript 134 // Auto-generate slugs 135 import { slugField } from 'payload' 136 slugField({ fieldToUse: 'title' }) 137 138 // Relationship with filtering 139 { 140 name: 'category', 141 type: 'relationship', 142 relationTo: 'categories', 143 filterOptions: { active: { equals: true } }, 144 } 145 146 // Conditional field 147 { 148 name: 'featuredImage', 149 type: 'upload', 150 relationTo: 'media', 151 admin: { 152 condition: (data) => data.featured === true, 153 }, 154 } 155 156 // Virtual field 157 { 158 name: 'fullName', 159 type: 'text', 160 virtual: true, 161 hooks: { 162 afterRead: [({ siblingData }) => `${siblingData.firstName} ${siblingData.lastName}`], 163 }, 164 } 165 ``` 166 167 ## CRITICAL SECURITY PATTERNS 168 169 ### 1. Local API Access Control (MOST IMPORTANT) 170 171 ```typescript 172 // ❌ SECURITY BUG: Access control bypassed 173 await payload.find({ 174 collection: 'posts', 175 user: someUser, // Ignored! Operation runs with ADMIN privileges 176 }) 177 178 // ✅ SECURE: Enforces user permissions 179 await payload.find({ 180 collection: 'posts', 181 user: someUser, 182 overrideAccess: false, // REQUIRED 183 }) 184 185 // ✅ Administrative operation (intentional bypass) 186 await payload.find({ 187 collection: 'posts', 188 // No user, overrideAccess defaults to true 189 }) 190 ``` 191 192 **Rule**: When passing `user` to Local API, ALWAYS set `overrideAccess: false` 193 194 ### 2. Transaction Safety in Hooks 195 196 ```typescript 197 // ❌ DATA CORRUPTION RISK: Separate transaction 198 hooks: { 199 afterChange: [ 200 async ({ doc, req }) => { 201 await req.payload.create({ 202 collection: 'audit-log', 203 data: { docId: doc.id }, 204 // Missing req - runs in separate transaction! 205 }) 206 }, 207 ], 208 } 209 210 // ✅ ATOMIC: Same transaction 211 hooks: { 212 afterChange: [ 213 async ({ doc, req }) => { 214 await req.payload.create({ 215 collection: 'audit-log', 216 data: { docId: doc.id }, 217 req, // Maintains atomicity 218 }) 219 }, 220 ], 221 } 222 ``` 223 224 **Rule**: ALWAYS pass `req` to nested operations in hooks 225 226 ### 3. Prevent Infinite Hook Loops 227 228 ```typescript 229 // ❌ INFINITE LOOP 230 hooks: { 231 afterChange: [ 232 async ({ doc, req }) => { 233 await req.payload.update({ 234 collection: 'posts', 235 id: doc.id, 236 data: { views: doc.views + 1 }, 237 req, 238 }) // Triggers afterChange again! 239 }, 240 ], 241 } 242 243 // ✅ SAFE: Use context flag 244 hooks: { 245 afterChange: [ 246 async ({ doc, req, context }) => { 247 if (context.skipHooks) return 248 249 await req.payload.update({ 250 collection: 'posts', 251 id: doc.id, 252 data: { views: doc.views + 1 }, 253 context: { skipHooks: true }, 254 req, 255 }) 256 }, 257 ], 258 } 259 ``` 260 261 ## Access Control 262 263 ### Collection-Level Access 264 265 ```typescript 266 import type { Access } from 'payload' 267 268 // Boolean return 269 const authenticated: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => Boolean(user) 270 271 // Query constraint (row-level security) 272 const ownPostsOnly: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => { 273 if (!user) return false 274 if (user?.roles?.includes('admin')) return true 275 276 return { 277 author: { equals: user.id }, 278 } 279 } 280 281 // Async access check 282 const projectMemberAccess: Access = async ({ req, id }) => { 283 const { user, payload } = req 284 285 if (!user) return false 286 if (user.roles?.includes('admin')) return true 287 288 const project = await payload.findByID({ 289 collection: 'projects', 290 id: id as string, 291 depth: 0, 292 }) 293 294 return project.members?.includes(user.id) 295 } 296 ``` 297 298 ### Field-Level Access 299 300 ```typescript 301 // Field access ONLY returns boolean (no query constraints) 302 { 303 name: 'salary', 304 type: 'number', 305 access: { 306 read: ({ req: { user }, doc }) => { 307 // Self can read own salary 308 if (user?.id === doc?.id) return true 309 // Admin can read all 310 return user?.roles?.includes('admin') 311 }, 312 update: ({ req: { user } }) => { 313 // Only admins can update 314 return user?.roles?.includes('admin') 315 }, 316 }, 317 } 318 ``` 319 320 ### Common Access Patterns 321 322 ```typescript 323 // Anyone 324 export const anyone: Access = () => true 325 326 // Authenticated only 327 export const authenticated: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => Boolean(user) 328 329 // Admin only 330 export const adminOnly: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => { 331 return user?.roles?.includes('admin') 332 } 333 334 // Admin or self 335 export const adminOrSelf: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => { 336 if (user?.roles?.includes('admin')) return true 337 return { id: { equals: user?.id } } 338 } 339 340 // Published or authenticated 341 export const authenticatedOrPublished: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => { 342 if (user) return true 343 return { _status: { equals: 'published' } } 344 } 345 ``` 346 347 ## Advanced Access Control Patterns 348 349 ### Context-Aware Access 350 351 ```typescript 352 // Locale-specific access 353 export const localeSpecificAccess: Access = ({ req: { user, locale } }) => { 354 if (user) return true 355 return locale === 'en' // Public users can only access English content 356 } 357 358 // IP-based access (factory function) 359 export function restrictedIpAccess(allowedIps: string[]): Access { 360 return ({ req: { headers } }) => { 361 const ip = headers?.get('x-forwarded-for') || headers?.get('x-real-ip') 362 return allowedIps.includes(ip || '') 363 } 364 } 365 ``` 366 367 ### Time-Based Access 368 369 ```typescript 370 // Recent records only (last N days) 371 export function recentRecordsAccess(days: number): Access { 372 return ({ req: { user } }) => { 373 if (!user) return false 374 if (user.roles?.includes('admin')) return true 375 376 const cutoff = new Date() 377 cutoff.setDate(cutoff.getDate() - days) 378 379 return { 380 createdAt: { greater_than_equal: cutoff.toISOString() }, 381 } 382 } 383 } 384 385 // Scheduled content (publish window) 386 export const scheduledContentAccess: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => { 387 if (user?.roles?.includes('admin') || user?.roles?.includes('editor')) { 388 return true 389 } 390 391 const now = new Date().toISOString() 392 return { 393 and: [ 394 { publishDate: { less_than_equal: now } }, 395 { 396 or: [{ unpublishDate: { exists: false } }, { unpublishDate: { greater_than: now } }], 397 }, 398 ], 399 } 400 } 401 ``` 402 403 ### Access Control Factory Functions 404 405 ```typescript 406 // Role-based factory 407 export function createRoleBasedAccess(roles: string[]): Access { 408 return ({ req: { user } }) => { 409 if (!user) return false 410 return roles.some((role) => user.roles?.includes(role)) 411 } 412 } 413 414 // Organization-scoped factory 415 export function createOrgScopedAccess(allowAdmin = true): Access { 416 return ({ req: { user } }) => { 417 if (!user) return false 418 if (allowAdmin && user.roles?.includes('admin')) return true 419 return { organizationId: { in: user.organizationIds || [] } } 420 } 421 } 422 423 // Time-limited factory 424 export function createTimeLimitedAccess(daysAccess: number): Access { 425 return ({ req: { user } }) => { 426 if (!user) return false 427 if (user.roles?.includes('admin')) return true 428 429 const cutoff = new Date() 430 cutoff.setDate(cutoff.getDate() - daysAccess) 431 return { createdAt: { greater_than_equal: cutoff.toISOString() } } 432 } 433 } 434 ``` 435 436 ### Access Control Performance 437 438 ```typescript 439 // ❌ Slow: Multiple async calls in access check 440 export const slowAccess: Access = async ({ req: { user } }) => { 441 const org = await req.payload.findByID({ collection: 'orgs', id: user.orgId }) 442 const team = await req.payload.findByID({ collection: 'teams', id: user.teamId }) 443 return org.active && team.active 444 } 445 446 // ✅ Fast: Use query constraints or cache in context 447 export const fastAccess: Access = ({ req: { user, context } }) => { 448 if (!context.orgStatus) { 449 context.orgStatus = checkOrgStatus(user.orgId) // Cache expensive lookups 450 } 451 return context.orgStatus 452 } 453 454 // ✅ Better: Use query constraint to filter at DB level 455 export const efficientAccess: Access = () => { 456 return { isPublic: { equals: true } } // Let database filter 457 } 458 ``` 459 460 ## Hooks 461 462 ### Common Hook Patterns 463 464 ```typescript 465 import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload' 466 467 export const Posts: CollectionConfig = { 468 slug: 'posts', 469 hooks: { 470 // Before validation - format data 471 beforeValidate: [ 472 async ({ data, operation }) => { 473 if (operation === 'create') { 474 data.slug = slugify(data.title) 475 } 476 return data 477 }, 478 ], 479 480 // Before save - business logic 481 beforeChange: [ 482 async ({ data, req, operation, originalDoc }) => { 483 if (operation === 'update' && data.status === 'published') { 484 data.publishedAt = new Date() 485 } 486 return data 487 }, 488 ], 489 490 // After save - side effects 491 afterChange: [ 492 async ({ doc, req, operation, previousDoc, context }) => { 493 // Check context to prevent loops 494 if (context.skipNotification) return 495 496 if (operation === 'create') { 497 await sendNotification(doc) 498 } 499 return doc 500 }, 501 ], 502 503 // After read - computed fields 504 afterRead: [ 505 async ({ doc, req }) => { 506 doc.viewCount = await getViewCount(doc.id) 507 return doc 508 }, 509 ], 510 511 // Before delete - cascading deletes 512 beforeDelete: [ 513 async ({ req, id }) => { 514 await req.payload.delete({ 515 collection: 'comments', 516 where: { post: { equals: id } }, 517 req, // Important for transaction 518 }) 519 }, 520 ], 521 }, 522 } 523 ``` 524 525 ## Queries 526 527 ### Local API 528 529 ```typescript 530 // Find with complex query 531 const posts = await payload.find({ 532 collection: 'posts', 533 where: { 534 and: [{ status: { equals: 'published' } }, { 'author.name': { contains: 'john' } }], 535 }, 536 depth: 2, // Populate relationships 537 limit: 10, 538 sort: '-createdAt', 539 select: { 540 title: true, 541 author: true, 542 }, 543 }) 544 545 // Find by ID 546 const post = await payload.findByID({ 547 collection: 'posts', 548 id: '123', 549 depth: 2, 550 }) 551 552 // Create 553 const newPost = await payload.create({ 554 collection: 'posts', 555 data: { 556 title: 'New Post', 557 status: 'draft', 558 }, 559 }) 560 561 // Update 562 await payload.update({ 563 collection: 'posts', 564 id: '123', 565 data: { status: 'published' }, 566 }) 567 568 // Delete 569 await payload.delete({ 570 collection: 'posts', 571 id: '123', 572 }) 573 ``` 574 575 ### Query Operators 576 577 ```typescript 578 // Equals 579 { status: { equals: 'published' } } 580 581 // Not equals 582 { status: { not_equals: 'draft' } } 583 584 // Greater than / less than 585 { price: { greater_than: 100 } } 586 { age: { less_than_equal: 65 } } 587 588 // Contains (case-insensitive) 589 { title: { contains: 'payload' } } 590 591 // Like (all words present) 592 { description: { like: 'cms headless' } } 593 594 // In array 595 { category: { in: ['tech', 'news'] } } 596 597 // Exists 598 { image: { exists: true } } 599 600 // Near (geospatial) 601 { location: { near: [-122.4194, 37.7749, 10000] } } 602 ``` 603 604 ### AND/OR Logic 605 606 ```typescript 607 { 608 or: [ 609 { status: { equals: 'published' } }, 610 { author: { equals: user.id } }, 611 ], 612 } 613 614 { 615 and: [ 616 { status: { equals: 'published' } }, 617 { featured: { equals: true } }, 618 ], 619 } 620 ``` 621 622 ## Getting Payload Instance 623 624 ```typescript 625 // In API routes (Next.js) 626 import { getPayload } from 'payload' 627 import config from '@payload-config' 628 629 export async function GET() { 630 const payload = await getPayload({ config }) 631 632 const posts = await payload.find({ 633 collection: 'posts', 634 }) 635 636 return Response.json(posts) 637 } 638 639 // In Server Components 640 import { getPayload } from 'payload' 641 import config from '@payload-config' 642 643 export default async function Page() { 644 const payload = await getPayload({ config }) 645 const { docs } = await payload.find({ collection: 'posts' }) 646 647 return
{docs.map(post =>

{post.title}

)}
648 } 649 ``` 650 651 ## Components 652 653 The Admin Panel can be extensively customized using React Components. Custom Components can be Server Components (default) or Client Components. 654 655 > **Note**: The component examples below demonstrate Payload CMS patterns and capabilities. They are illustrative examples showing what's possible, not documentation of components in this specific project. See `src/ui/` for actual implementations in this project. 656 657 ### Defining Components 658 659 Components are defined using **file paths** (not direct imports) in your config: 660 661 **Component Path Rules:** 662 663 - Paths are relative to project root or `config.admin.importMap.baseDir` 664 - Named exports: use `#ExportName` suffix or `exportName` property 665 - Default exports: no suffix needed 666 - File extensions can be omitted 667 668 ```typescript 669 import { buildConfig } from 'payload' 670 671 export default buildConfig({ 672 admin: { 673 components: { 674 // Logo and branding 675 graphics: { 676 Logo: '/components/Logo', 677 Icon: '/components/Icon', 678 }, 679 680 // Navigation 681 Nav: '/components/CustomNav', 682 beforeNavLinks: ['/components/CustomNavItem'], 683 afterNavLinks: ['/components/NavFooter'], 684 685 // Header 686 header: ['/components/AnnouncementBanner'], 687 actions: ['/components/ClearCache', '/components/Preview'], 688 689 // Dashboard 690 beforeDashboard: ['/components/WelcomeMessage'], 691 afterDashboard: ['/components/Analytics'], 692 693 // Auth 694 beforeLogin: ['/components/SSOButtons'], 695 logout: { Button: '/components/LogoutButton' }, 696 697 // Settings 698 settingsMenu: ['/components/SettingsMenu'], 699 700 // Views 701 views: { 702 dashboard: { Component: '/components/CustomDashboard' }, 703 }, 704 }, 705 }, 706 }) 707 ``` 708 709 **Component Path Rules:** 710 711 - Paths are relative to project root or `config.admin.importMap.baseDir` 712 - Named exports: use `#ExportName` suffix or `exportName` property 713 - Default exports: no suffix needed 714 - File extensions can be omitted 715 716 ### Component Types 717 718 1. **Root Components** - Global Admin Panel (logo, nav, header) 719 2. **Collection Components** - Collection-specific (edit view, list view) 720 3. **Global Components** - Global document views 721 4. **Field Components** - Custom field UI and cells 722 723 ### Component Types 724 725 1. **Root Components** - Global Admin Panel (logo, nav, header) 726 2. **Collection Components** - Collection-specific (edit view, list view) 727 3. **Global Components** - Global document views 728 4. **Field Components** - Custom field UI and cells 729 730 ### Server vs Client Components 731 732 **All components are Server Components by default** (can use Local API directly): 733 734 ```tsx 735 // Server Component (default) 736 import type { Payload } from 'payload' 737 738 async function MyServerComponent({ payload }: { payload: Payload }) { 739 const posts = await payload.find({ collection: 'posts' }) 740 return
{posts.totalDocs} posts
741 } 742 743 export default MyServerComponent 744 ``` 745 746 **Client Components** need the `'use client'` directive: 747 748 ```tsx 749 'use client' 750 import { useState } from 'react' 751 import { useAuth } from '@payloadcms/ui' 752 753 export function MyClientComponent() { 754 const [count, setCount] = useState(0) 755 const { user } = useAuth() 756 757 return ( 758 761 ) 762 } 763 ``` 764 765 ### Using Hooks (Client Components Only) 766 767 ```tsx 768 'use client' 769 import { 770 useAuth, // Current user 771 useConfig, // Payload config (client-safe) 772 useDocumentInfo, // Document info (id, collection, etc.) 773 useField, // Field value and setter 774 useForm, // Form state 775 useFormFields, // Multiple field values (optimized) 776 useLocale, // Current locale 777 useTranslation, // i18n translations 778 usePayload, // Local API methods 779 } from '@payloadcms/ui' 780 781 export function MyComponent() { 782 const { user } = useAuth() 783 const { config } = useConfig() 784 const { id, collection } = useDocumentInfo() 785 const locale = useLocale() 786 const { t } = useTranslation() 787 788 return
Hello {user?.email}
789 } 790 ``` 791 792 ### Collection/Global Components 793 794 ```typescript 795 export const Posts: CollectionConfig = { 796 slug: 'posts', 797 admin: { 798 components: { 799 // Edit view 800 edit: { 801 PreviewButton: '/components/PostPreview', 802 SaveButton: '/components/CustomSave', 803 SaveDraftButton: '/components/SaveDraft', 804 PublishButton: '/components/Publish', 805 }, 806 807 // List view 808 list: { 809 Header: '/components/ListHeader', 810 beforeList: ['/components/BulkActions'], 811 afterList: ['/components/ListFooter'], 812 }, 813 }, 814 }, 815 } 816 ``` 817 818 ### Field Components 819 820 ```typescript 821 { 822 name: 'status', 823 type: 'select', 824 options: ['draft', 'published'], 825 admin: { 826 components: { 827 // Edit view field 828 Field: '/components/StatusField', 829 // List view cell 830 Cell: '/components/StatusCell', 831 // Field label 832 Label: '/components/StatusLabel', 833 // Field description 834 Description: '/components/StatusDescription', 835 // Error message 836 Error: '/components/StatusError', 837 }, 838 }, 839 } 840 ``` 841 842 **UI Field** (presentational only, no data): 843 844 ```typescript 845 { 846 name: 'refundButton', 847 type: 'ui', 848 admin: { 849 components: { 850 Field: '/components/RefundButton', 851 }, 852 }, 853 } 854 ``` 855 856 ### Performance Best Practices 857 858 1. **Import correctly:** 859 - Admin Panel: `import { Button } from '@payloadcms/ui'` 860 - Frontend: `import { Button } from '@payloadcms/ui/elements/Button'` 861 862 2. **Optimize re-renders:** 863 864 ```tsx 865 // ❌ BAD: Re-renders on every form change 866 const { fields } = useForm() 867 868 // ✅ GOOD: Only re-renders when specific field changes 869 const value = useFormFields(([fields]) => fields[path]) 870 ``` 871 872 3. **Prefer Server Components** - Only use Client Components when you need: 873 - State (useState, useReducer) 874 - Effects (useEffect) 875 - Event handlers (onClick, onChange) 876 - Browser APIs (localStorage, window) 877 878 4. **Minimize serialized props** - Server Components serialize props sent to client 879 880 ### Styling Components 881 882 This project uses **Tailwind CSS** with a **semantic design token system**. See `.kody/memory/design-system.md` for complete rules. 883 884 **Key rules:** 885 886 - Use semantic typography tokens (`text-body-sm`, `text-heading-lg`, `text-display-md`) — never `text-sm`, `text-xl`, `text-4xl` 887 - Use Tailwind color utilities (`bg-primary`, `text-success`) — never `[hsl(var(--xxx))]` or hardcoded colors (`text-red-500`) 888 - Use shadow tokens (`shadow-elevation-1`, `shadow-card`) — never `shadow-sm`, `shadow-md` 889 - Use spacing tokens for layout (`p-card-padding`, `gap-content-gap`) — never `p-6`, `gap-8` 890 - All interactive elements need `transition-all duration-normal` 891 - Use `cn()` for className composition — never template literals 892 - Never use inline `style={{}}` for typography or colors 893 894 ```tsx 895 // CORRECT 896 export function MyComponent() { 897 return ( 898
899 Content 900
901 ) 902 } 903 ``` 904 905 For Payload admin components, you can use Payload's CSS variables via Tailwind's arbitrary value syntax: 906 907 ```tsx 908
Admin content
909 ``` 910 911 **Project Convention**: Place components alongside their related feature code: 912 913 - Admin components: `src/ui/admin/MyComponent/index.tsx` 914 - Web components: `src/ui/web/MyComponent/index.tsx` 915 916 ### Type Safety 917 918 ```tsx 919 import type { 920 TextFieldServerComponent, 921 TextFieldClientComponent, 922 TextFieldCellComponent, 923 SelectFieldServerComponent, 924 // ... etc 925 } from 'payload' 926 927 export const MyField: TextFieldClientComponent = (props) => { 928 // Fully typed props 929 } 930 ``` 931 932 ### Import Map 933 934 Payload auto-generates `app/(payload)/admin/importMap.js` to resolve component paths. 935 936 **Regenerate manually:** 937 938 ```bash 939 payload generate:importmap 940 ``` 941 942 **Set custom location:** 943 944 ```typescript 945 export default buildConfig({ 946 admin: { 947 importMap: { 948 baseDir: path.resolve(dirname, 'src'), 949 importMapFile: path.resolve(dirname, 'app', 'custom-import-map.js'), 950 }, 951 }, 952 }) 953 ``` 954 955 ### Project-Specific Components 956 957 This project includes the following custom components in `src/ui/`: 958 959 - **AdminBar** - Frontend admin controls with editing shortcuts 960 - **BeforeDashboard** - Dashboard header with seed button 961 - **BeforeLogin** - Login page customizations 962 - **Card** - Reusable card component 963 - **CollectionArchive** - Archive list views 964 - **Link** - Custom link component 965 - **LivePreviewListener** - Live preview functionality 966 - **Logo** - Custom branding logo 967 - **Media** - Media rendering components 968 - **PageRange** - Pagination range display 969 - **Pagination** - Pagination controls 970 - **PayloadRedirects** - Client-side redirect handling 971 - **RichText** - Rich text rendering 972 - **ui/** - Shadcn/UI components (button, card, checkbox, input, label, pagination, select, textarea) 973 974 ## Custom Endpoints 975 976 ```typescript 977 import type { Endpoint } from 'payload' 978 import { APIError } from 'payload' 979 980 // Always check authentication 981 export const protectedEndpoint: Endpoint = { 982 path: '/protected', 983 method: 'get', 984 handler: async (req) => { 985 if (!req.user) { 986 throw new APIError('Unauthorized', 401) 987 } 988 989 // Use req.payload for database operations 990 const data = await req.payload.find({ 991 collection: 'posts', 992 where: { author: { equals: req.user.id } }, 993 }) 994 995 return Response.json(data) 996 }, 997 } 998 999 // Route parameters 1000 export const trackingEndpoint: Endpoint = { 1001 path: '/:id/tracking', 1002 method: 'get', 1003 handler: async (req) => { 1004 const { id } = req.routeParams 1005 1006 const tracking = await getTrackingInfo(id) 1007 1008 if (!tracking) { 1009 return Response.json({ error: 'not found' }, { status: 404 }) 1010 } 1011 1012 return Response.json(tracking) 1013 }, 1014 } 1015 ``` 1016 1017 ## Drafts & Versions 1018 1019 ```typescript 1020 export const Pages: CollectionConfig = { 1021 slug: 'pages', 1022 versions: { 1023 drafts: { 1024 autosave: true, 1025 schedulePublish: true, 1026 validate: false, // Don't validate drafts 1027 }, 1028 maxPerDoc: 100, 1029 }, 1030 access: { 1031 read: ({ req: { user } }) => { 1032 // Public sees only published 1033 if (!user) return { _status: { equals: 'published' } } 1034 // Authenticated sees all 1035 return true 1036 }, 1037 }, 1038 } 1039 1040 // Create draft 1041 await payload.create({ 1042 collection: 'pages', 1043 data: { title: 'Draft Page' }, 1044 draft: true, // Skips required field validation 1045 }) 1046 1047 // Read with drafts 1048 const page = await payload.findByID({ 1049 collection: 'pages', 1050 id: '123', 1051 draft: true, // Returns draft if available 1052 }) 1053 ``` 1054 1055 ## Field Type Guards 1056 1057 ```typescript 1058 import { 1059 fieldAffectsData, 1060 fieldHasSubFields, 1061 fieldIsArrayType, 1062 fieldIsBlockType, 1063 fieldSupportsMany, 1064 fieldHasMaxDepth, 1065 } from 'payload' 1066 1067 function processField(field: Field) { 1068 // Check if field stores data 1069 if (fieldAffectsData(field)) { 1070 console.log(field.name) // Safe to access 1071 } 1072 1073 // Check if field has nested fields 1074 if (fieldHasSubFields(field)) { 1075 field.fields.forEach(processField) // Safe to access 1076 } 1077 1078 // Check field type 1079 if (fieldIsArrayType(field)) { 1080 console.log(field.minRows, field.maxRows) 1081 } 1082 1083 // Check capabilities 1084 if (fieldSupportsMany(field) && field.hasMany) { 1085 console.log('Multiple values supported') 1086 } 1087 } 1088 ``` 1089 1090 ## Plugins 1091 1092 ### Using Plugins 1093 1094 ```typescript 1095 import { seoPlugin } from '@payloadcms/plugin-seo' 1096 import { redirectsPlugin } from '@payloadcms/plugin-redirects' 1097 1098 export default buildConfig({ 1099 plugins: [ 1100 seoPlugin({ 1101 collections: ['posts', 'pages'], 1102 }), 1103 redirectsPlugin({ 1104 collections: ['pages'], 1105 }), 1106 ], 1107 }) 1108 ``` 1109 1110 ### Creating Plugins 1111 1112 ```typescript 1113 import type { Config, Plugin } from 'payload' 1114 1115 interface MyPluginConfig { 1116 collections?: string[] 1117 enabled?: boolean 1118 } 1119 1120 export const myPlugin = 1121 (options: MyPluginConfig): Plugin => 1122 (config: Config): Config => ({ 1123 ...config, 1124 collections: config.collections?.map((collection) => { 1125 if (options.collections?.includes(collection.slug)) { 1126 return { 1127 ...collection, 1128 fields: [...collection.fields, { name: 'pluginField', type: 'text' }], 1129 } 1130 } 1131 return collection 1132 }), 1133 }) 1134 ``` 1135 1136 ## Best Practices 1137 1138 ### Security 1139 1140 1. Always set `overrideAccess: false` when passing `user` to Local API 1141 2. Field-level access only returns boolean (no query constraints) 1142 3. Default to restrictive access, gradually add permissions 1143 4. Never trust client-provided data 1144 5. Use `saveToJWT: true` for roles to avoid database lookups 1145 1146 ### Performance 1147 1148 1. Index frequently queried fields 1149 2. Use `select` to limit returned fields 1150 3. Set `maxDepth` on relationships to prevent over-fetching 1151 4. Use query constraints over async operations in access control 1152 5. Cache expensive operations in `req.context` 1153 1154 ### Data Integrity 1155 1156 1. Always pass `req` to nested operations in hooks 1157 2. Use context flags to prevent infinite hook loops 1158 3. Enable transactions for MongoDB (requires replica set) 1159 4. Use `beforeValidate` for data formatting 1160 5. Use `beforeChange` for business logic 1161 1162 ### Type Safety 1163 1164 1. Run `generate:types` after schema changes 1165 2. Import types from generated `payload-types.ts` 1166 3. Type your user object: `import type { User } from '@/payload-types'` 1167 4. Use `as const` for field options 1168 5. Use field type guards for runtime type checking 1169 1170 ### Organization 1171 1172 1. Keep collections in separate files 1173 2. Extract access control to `access/` directory 1174 3. Extract hooks to `hooks/` directory 1175 4. Use reusable field factories for common patterns 1176 5. Document complex access control with comments 1177 1178 ## Common Gotchas 1179 1180 1. **Local API Default**: Access control bypassed unless `overrideAccess: false` 1181 2. **Transaction Safety**: Missing `req` in nested operations breaks atomicity 1182 3. **Hook Loops**: Operations in hooks can trigger the same hooks 1183 4. **Field Access**: Cannot use query constraints, only boolean 1184 5. **Relationship Depth**: Default depth is 2, set to 0 for IDs only 1185 6. **Draft Status**: `_status` field auto-injected when drafts enabled 1186 7. **Type Generation**: Types not updated until `generate:types` runs 1187 8. **MongoDB Transactions**: Require replica set configuration 1188 9. **Local Filesystem**: Do NOT use `upload: { staticDir: ... }` - use Vercel Blob instead 1189 1190 ## Additional Documentation 1191 1192 For deeper exploration of specific topics, refer to the README files throughout the project: 1193 1194 | Topic | Location | 1195 | ---------------- | --------------------------------- | 1196 | Access Control | `docs/access-control/README.md` | 1197 | Admin Components | `docs/admin-components/README.md` | 1198 | AI Services | `docs/ai-services/README.md` | 1199 | Block Rendering | `docs/block-rendering/README.md` | 1200 | Collections | `src/server/payload/collections/` | 1201 | Components | `src/ui/` | 1202 | Course Hierarchy | `docs/course-hierarchy/README.md` | 1203 | Exercises | `docs/exercises/README.md` | 1204 | Exercise Import | `docs/exercise-import/README.md` | 1205 | Testing | `tests/README.md` | 1206 1207 ### Pattern Discovery 1208 1209 Find code examples using the AI-optimized indexes: 1210 1211 - **Pattern Index**: `.ai-docs/indexes/pattern-index.json` - 208 files × 24 patterns 1212 - **README Index**: `.ai-docs/readme-index.json` - 19 READMEs indexed 1213 - **Doc Chunks**: `.ai-docs/indexes/doc-chunks.json` - 248 searchable chunks 1214 1215 ## AI Agent Optimization 1216 1217 This codebase includes tools to help AI agents work efficiently: 1218 1219 - **SmartDocLoader**: Context-aware documentation loading that minimizes token usage 1220 - **DocSearch**: Fast keyword-based documentation search 1221 - **Pattern Index**: Pattern → files mapping for quick example discovery 1222 - **JSON Schemas**: Machine-readable validation contracts for code generation 1223 - **Centralized Types**: Common types exported from `src/types/index.ts` for easy discovery 1224 1225 ### Quick Start 1226 1227 ```typescript 1228 // Load context-aware documentation 1229 import { SmartDocLoader } from '@/lib/ai/smart-doc-loader' 1230 1231 const docs = SmartDocLoader.forCollection('create') 1232 // Returns: ~380 tokens, quick reference tier 1233 1234 // Search documentation 1235 import { getDocSearch } from '@/lib/ai/doc-search' 1236 const search = getDocSearch() 1237 const results = search.query('How do I create a published collection?') 1238 1239 // Use centralized types 1240 import type { User, Course, CollectionConfig } from '@/types' 1241 ``` 1242 1243 See [docs/ai/README.md](./docs/ai/README.md) for full guide and [BOOTSTRAP.md](.ai-docs/BOOTSTRAP.md) for quick reference. 1244 1245 ## Resources 1246 1247 - Docs: https://payloadcms.com/docs 1248 - LLM Context: https://payloadcms.com/llms-full.txt 1249 - GitHub: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload 1250 - Examples: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/examples 1251 - Templates: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/templates 1252 1253 --- 1254 1255 ## Kody memory protocol 1256 1257 You have a persistent memory at `.kody/memory/`. `INDEX.md` is the cheap 1258 table of contents; read it at the start of any session and read individual 1259 memory files only when they look relevant. Never edit memory files 1260 directly — they are owned by the `memory-writer` job. 1261 1262 ### When to record a memory 1263 1264 - **Preference** — you learn how the operator wants kody to behave. 1265 - **Decision** — a non-obvious choice was made (and especially _why_). 1266 - **Lesson** — you tried something that broke or surprised you; future 1267 sessions should not repeat it. 1268 - **Verdict** — an inbox approve / reject / dismiss happened (dashboard 1269 writes these; you only do it from a chat where the operator gave a 1270 verdict in words). 1271 1272 If the thing is already in the code, the commit history, or current 1273 session context, don't bother — memory is for things that would 1274 otherwise evaporate. 1275 1276 ### How to record a memory 1277 1278 Drop a single JSON sticky note in `.kody/memory/inbox/`. The 1279 `memory-writer` job picks it up on its next tick (every 15m) and files 1280 it under `.kody/memory/.md` with frontmatter, updating 1281 `INDEX.md`. 1282 1283 Sticky note shape (see `.kody/memory/inbox/README.md` for the full 1284 contract): 1285 1286 ```json 1287 { 1288 "type": "preference | decision | lesson | verdict | architecture", 1289 "name": "kebab-case-slug", 1290 "title": "Human-readable title for INDEX.md", 1291 "hook": "One-line hook for INDEX.md", 1292 "body": "Full markdown content — no frontmatter.", 1293 "source": "chat | executor: | job: | dashboard:", 1294 "ts": "", 1295 "links": ["other-memory-slug"] 1296 } 1297 ``` 1298 1299 Filename: `--.json` — must be unique so 1300 parallel writers never collide. 1301 1302 Commit the sticky note (and only the sticky note) when you record. Do 1303 **not** also write `.kody/memory/.md` yourself — that is the 1304 writer job's territory and editing both at once will cause merge 1305 conflicts on the next tick. 1306 1307 ### What not to record 1308 1309 - The current shape of the code (it's in the code). 1310 - A summary of what you just committed (it's in `git log`). 1311 - Ephemeral task state (already in `*.state.json`). 1312 - Information already documented in `AGENTS.md` or `CLAUDE.md`. 1313

payload.text: Now I understand the memory protocol from AGENTS.md. Let me check the existing inbox structure and the README for the full contract.

payload.content:
1	# Memory sticky-note inbox
2	
3	Writers drop one JSON file per memory they want recorded. The `memory-writer`
4	job drains this folder on its tick, files each note under `.kody/memory/`,
5	updates `INDEX.md`, and deletes the processed sticky note.
6	
7	## File shape
8	
9	Filename: `<utc-iso>-<source>-<short-uuid>.json` — must be unique so parallel
10	writers never collide.
11	
12	Example: `2026-05-20T14-03-22Z-chat-abc123.json`
13	
14	Body:
15	
16	```json
17	{
18	  "type": "preference | decision | lesson | verdict | architecture",
19	  "name": "kebab-case-slug",
20	  "title": "Human-readable title for INDEX.md",
21	  "hook": "One-line hook for INDEX.md",
22	  "body": "Full markdown content for the memory file (no frontmatter — the job adds it).",
23	  "source": "chat | executor:<executable> | dashboard:<action> | job:<slug>",
24	  "ts": "2026-05-20T14:03:22Z",
25	  "links": ["other-memory-slug"]
26	}
27	```
28	
29	## Rules
30	
31	- Never edit an existing sticky note — drop a new one if you change your mind.
32	- Never write to `.kody/memory/*.md` directly from a writer. The job owns those.
33	- A sticky note with the same `name` as an existing memory file is treated as
34	  an update (the job overwrites the body and refreshes the INDEX line).
35	- Sticky notes are transient. After processing, the job deletes them. The
36	  permanent record is the memory file plus the git history of this folder.
37

payload.text:
Now I have everything I need. Let me create the sticky note JSON file. The user wants:
- type=lesson
- name=writer1-chat-proof
- body explaining this is the first chat-driven memory written by an LLM session following the prose contract in AGENTS.md

I need to:
1. Generate a filename: `<utc-iso>-<source>-<short-uuid>.json`
   - source would be "chat" since it's from a chat
   - I'll generate a short uuid
2. Create the JSON content
3. Write the file
4. Commit it

For the timestamp, today's date is 2026-05-20.

input.content:
{
  "type": "lesson",
  "name": "writer1-chat-proof",
  "title": "First chat-driven memory written by LLM session",
  "hook": "First LLM-authored sticky note following prose contract in AGENTS.md",
  "body": "This is the first chat-driven memory written entirely by an LLM session using the prose contract defined in AGENTS.md. The sticky note was created via chat (source=chat), dropped into .kody/memory/inbox/, and will be processed by the memory-writer job on its next tick.",
  "source": "chat",
  "ts": "2026-05-20T00:00:01Z",
  "links": []
}

payload.text: Now I need to commit just this file.

input.command:
git add .kody/memory/inbox/2026-05-20T00-00-01Z-chat-000001.json && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(memory): Add first chat-driven LLM memory sticky note

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
